inputs.titre: Turgot nommé Contrôleur général des finances

reason: The image depicts Turgot in a palatial setting, appearing to be appointed Controller-General of Finances in 1774. The clothing (justacorps, waistcoat, ruffled shirt), furniture (writing desk, side table), and architectural details (draperies, gilded moldings, stone walls) are consistent with the late 18th century. Lighting by candles and natural light through the window creates an appropriate atmosphere. There are no immediately visible anachronisms or fantastical elements. However, the modern photography gives a more detailed render than would have existed at that time.
